Super League season}} From 2026, the [[Super League]] expanded to 14 teams, with [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[York Knights]] being promoted from the [[RFL Championship]] via the [[IMG (company)|IMG]] [[IMG Grading for the British Rugby Football League|Grading system]].{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=York and Toulouse to play in expanded Super League |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cly24zvxw3no |access-date=17 October 2025 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=17 October 2025}} At a meeting of all twelve Super League teams at the [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] on 28 July 2025 to vote on the expansion proposal, Hull F.C. were one of two teams to vote against expanding the league, with the club stating they were against any expansion of the Super League at any time in the current economic climate.{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=The three clubs who did not vote for Super League expansion revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/the-three-clubs-who-were-against-super-league-expansion-revealed |access-date=11 October 2025 |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=28 July 2025}} ===Fixtures=== [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] | | – | | | | [[Sky Sports]] | | |} ===Table=== {{2026 Super League regular season table|showteam=HUL}} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2026 Challenge Cup}} Hull F.C. were first drawn on 12 January to play the newly-reformed [[Salford RLFC]] away in Round 3,{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=Hull KR handed amateur trip as Challenge Cup third-round draw revealed |url=https://www.totalrl.com/challenge-cup-third-round-draw-revealed-2/ |access-date=12 January 2026 |work=Total Rugby League |date=12 January 2026}} following Salford's 42-10 defeat of [[Hammersmith Hills Hoists]] in their Round 2 fixture on 24 January.{{cite news |last1=Hayton |first1=Edward |title=Salford RLFC beat Hammersmith Hills Hoists 42-10 to set up Hull FC tie |url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2026/01/25/salford-rlfc-beat-hammersmith-hills-hoists-42-10-to-set-up-hull-fc-tie/ |access-date=25 January 2026 |work=Salford Now |date=25 January 2026}} Following their victory against Salford, Hull F.C. were drawn to play the [[Leigh Leopards]] away in Round 4.{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Daniel |title=Challenge Cup fourth-round draw as Hull FC and Hull KR both face away ties |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-fourth-round-draw-10804506 |access-date=9 February 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=9 February 2026}} Hull F.C. were ultimately knocked out of the 2026 Challenge Cup by Leigh in this fixture.{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Daniel |title=Poor Hull FC beaten once again as poor start to season continues in cup defeat |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match-reports/poor-hull-fc-beaten-once-10867387 |access-date=14 March 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=14 March 2026}}
